revenue.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2025 revenue source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

profitOrSurplus.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2025 profit or surplus source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

caveats: This row is total player compensation, not clean competition prize money.

The official US Open release confirms $90 million in total player compensation and $5 million in travel and hotel support; the separate clean competition-prize-money row excludes that support component.
The $90 million total includes support and expense coverage, so it is excluded from revenue and profit/surplus ratios.
US Open revenue and profit/surplus remain unavailable because no tournament-specific financial denominator is included.
USTA organization-level financials must remain separate from US Open tournament denominators.

prizeMoneyScope.notes: Total listed US Open 2024 competition-event payouts for staged singles, doubles, qualifying, and mixed-doubles events. Derived from the official payout breakdown and kept separate from the official $75m total player-compensation headline.

prizePool.notes: Derived by weighting the official 2024 US Open main draw singles, main draw doubles, qualifying singles, and mixed doubles payout tables across the listed draw stages. The official release reports $75m total player compensation; the secondary cross-check separates $68.756m in listed event payouts from $6.244m per diem.

revenue.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2024 revenue source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

profitOrSurplus.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2024 profit or surplus source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

caveats: Prize-money numerator is the derived subtotal of listed US Open 2024 competition-event payouts, $68.756m, not the official $75m total player-compensation headline.

The official US Open release reports $75m in total player compensation and lists the event payout schedule; it does not provide as explicit a support split as the 2025 release, so this row is medium confidence.
Secondary cross-check separates $6.244m in per diem from the $75m total; broader compensation/support remains outside this clean numerator.
US Open revenue and profit/surplus remain unavailable because no tournament-specific compatible financial denominator is included.
USTA organization-level financials must remain separate from US Open tournament denominators unless a source bridges the scope explicitly.

profitOrSurplus.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2024 profit or surplus source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

caveats: This row is total player compensation, not clean competition prize money.

prizeMoneyScope.notes: Total listed US Open 2022 competition-event payouts across singles, doubles, mixed doubles, qualifying, and wheelchair events. Derived from the archived official prize-money page plus a secondary split that separates per diem from competition payouts.

prizePool.notes: The archived official US Open 2022 prize-money page URL and official page title were verified in-browser, but the archive did not expose usable body text in this environment. The secondary split cites the official page and reports $56.4981m in listed professional event payouts, $1.032m in wheelchair event payouts, $2.5719m in per diem, and $60.102m total prize money; the clean row excludes the per-diem component.

revenue.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2022 revenue source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

profitOrSurplus.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2022 profit or surplus source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

caveats: Prize-money numerator is the medium-confidence US Open 2022 competition-event payout subtotal of $57.5301m, not the $60.102m total prize-money headline.
The archived official prize-money page was identified, but its body was not readable in this environment; the per-diem separation comes from a secondary split citing the official page and ATP.
Secondary cross-check separates $2.5719m in per diem from the $60.102m total; per diem remains outside this clean numerator.
US Open revenue and profit/surplus remain unavailable because no tournament-specific compatible financial denominator is included.
USTA organization-level financials must remain separate from US Open tournament denominators unless a source bridges the scope explicitly.

revenue.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2022 revenue source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

prizeMoneyScope.notes: Total listed US Open 2021 competition-event payouts across singles, doubles, mixed doubles, qualifying, and other competition events. Derived from the official total-player-compensation release plus a secondary split that separates estimated per diem from competition payouts.

prizePool.notes: The official US Open release reports a rounded $57.5m in 2021 prize money and total player compensation, nearly $6m for qualifying, and prize-money increases across singles, doubles, mixed doubles, and wheelchair competitions. The secondary split reports $53.75944m in listed event payouts, $0.600m in other competition events, $3.10256m in estimated per diem, and $57.462m total; the clean row excludes the per-diem component.

revenue.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2021 revenue source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

profitOrSurplus.notes: No clear tournament-specific US Open 2021 profit or surplus source is included; USTA organization-level financials are not treated as tournament denominators.

caveats: Prize-money numerator is the medium-confidence US Open 2021 competition-event payout subtotal of $54.35944m, not the official rounded $57.5m total-player-compensation headline.
The official release anchors total player compensation and competition categories, but the per-diem separation comes from a secondary split; this row remains medium confidence.
Secondary cross-check separates $3.10256m in estimated per diem from the $57.462m total; per diem remains outside this clean numerator.
US Open revenue and profit/surplus remain unavailable because no tournament-specific compatible financial denominator is included.
USTA organization-level financials must remain separate from US Open tournament denominators unless a source bridges the scope explicitly.
